title: Aluminum Prices Hit 6-Month High as Supply Concerns Grow
summary: Aluminum prices reached a 6-month high on the LME as emerging deficit concerns due to Chinese output nearing its production cap drove a breakout from a sideways trend. The Midwest Premium also joined the rally, hitting a new all-time high of $0.7323 per pound.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: The current price of Aluminium futures is 3,193.60, with a previous close of 3,199.00, reflecting minor intraday volatility in the market. The daily trading range shows stability around the 3,199.00 level.

title: Aluminium price
summary: On June 26, 2026, the aluminium price was recorded at 1.1399, equivalent to $3,176, with a monthly average of $3,469. The lowest value in the period was $3,146, indicating a slight downward trend from the monthly average.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers have developed a new nanofiltration process that selectively captures more than 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams generated during aluminum production. The captured aluminum can be upcycled and added to bulk aluminum, increasing yield while significantly reducing waste and environmental impact[1].

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights three key innovations: Hydro's HalZero technology that eliminates CO2 emissions during electrolysis by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop, U.S. research on removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum to expand its applications, and Rio Tinto's evaluation of hydrogen as an alternative to natural gas in alumina refining[2].

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
summary: Recycling aluminum uses approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions. The article emphasizes aluminum's role in enhancing efficiency and sustainability across all vehicle segments through advanced manufacturing and recycling practices[5].

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: The video discusses how rising diesel prices, up 50% since the start of the Iran war, are impacting steel shipping costs via truck and rail. Analysts note that steel is currently in a nine-month pricing upcycle near $1,100, with potential to reach $1,500 for hot rolled coil[2].

summary: Today's trading range for US Steel Coil futures is between 1,153.00 and 1,158.00, reflecting current market volatility in the US Midwest[3].

title: What the Data Says: Steel Price Updates
summary: Steel prices have tumbled by 2.85%, continuing a long-term trend where prices have plummeted over 16.5% in recent periods[7].

title: U.S. Raw Steel Production Rises in 2025! | ScrapMonster News
summary: The American Iron and Steel Institute (AISI) reported a 0.70% increase in U.S. raw steel production for the week ending January 25, 2025. This marks a positive trend in domestic steel output despite broader market uncertainties.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: Capacity utilization in the U.S. steel market slipped to 74.6%, below the administration's 80% goal, though recent indicators show stabilization. Seasonally adjusted output in April 2025 was 24.2 million net tons, down 1.3% from 2024, but mill capability utilization rose to 77.5% in May.

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: Global crude steelmaking capacity in 2023 reached 2,432 million tonnes, exceeding production by 543 million tonnes. Between 2024 and 2026, an additional 68.3 million tonnes of capacity is expected to come online, with potential additions of 88.7 million tonnes from announced plans.

title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand
summary: Twenty-one million tons of new steel production capacity have been announced or completed in the past four years, signaling a major expansion in U.S. steelmaking. However, this growth outpaces current demand, raising concerns about market sustainability without new clientele.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: In the week ending June 20, 2026, U.S. domestic raw steel production reached 1,851,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 80.2%. This indicates strong operational performance and near-target capacity usage in the domestic steel industry.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup that raised $50 million to develop a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis. It also details Sweden's $3.99 billion investment in a commercial hydrogen steel plant and Kobe Steel's new technique for reducing coke usage in natural gas blast furnaces.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: The report outlines emerging technologies like direct electrification and hydrogen-fueled processes that are heating up the steel market to achieve near-zero emissions. It emphasizes innovations in waste stream valorization, such as GreenOre using steel slag to create supplementary cementitious materials, and the production of the first commercial near-zero emissions steel in Northern Sweden.

title: Innovations in Heat & Water Recovery for a Greener Industry
summary: This video demonstrates how the Heat Pipe Condensing Economiser (HPCE) technology is transforming the steel sector to become more sustainable and economically viable by improving heat and water recovery. The technology aims to reduce energy consumption and emissions in industrial steel production processes.

title: The Future of Steel: Innovation and Sustainability with U. S. Steel
summary: United States Steel Corporation unveils the pivotal role of steel in modern manufacturing and its evolving path toward sustainability through innovation. The content explores how U.S. Steel is integrating new technologies to reduce environmental impact while maintaining production efficiency.

This price translates to approximately $12.18 per kilogram, highlighting copper's strong performance in the mineral mining sector[1].

title: Copper Prices Outlook | J.P. Morgan Global Research
summary: Copper prices have rebounded to approximately $13,000/mt amid a fluid geopolitical backdrop, though medium-term support is projected between $11,100–$11,200/mt. The report notes that Chinese micro fundamentals are improving even as copper remains exposed to supply-demand imbalances.

title: Record-breaking copper prices amid speculations of supply shortages
summary: Copper prices are expected to rise in the short and medium term as demand continues to grow at a higher pace than supply, with shortages anticipated due to copper ore constraints. Prices have surged over 20% since mid-February 2024, reaching a two-year peak of nearly USD 10,000 per tonne.

title: soaring copper demand an obstacle to future growth
summary: Copper demand is projected to climb from 1.7 Mtpa today to 4.3 Mtpa by 2035, requiring over eight Mtpa of new mine capacity and 3.5 Mtpa of additional scrap to meet growth. The shift to renewable energy systems will need an additional two Mtpa of copper supply over the next decade.

title: Copper prices drop amid global growth fears, plump stocks
summary: Copper prices have fallen 9.4% since February 27 as global growth fears weigh on the market, with inventories surpassing 1 million mt for the first time since 2003. The price drop reflects concerns over tighter US Federal Reserve policy and weakening global demand expectations.

title: Cobre Las Cruces es pionera en hidrometalurgia y procesos sostenibles
summary: Cobre Las Cruces lidera la transformación de minerales en metales críticos mediante su proyecto de refinería polimetalúrgica (PMR), que representa un cambio completo del modelo minero-metalúrgico tradicional. El proyecto tiene un impacto económico estimado de al menos 2.500 millones de euros en las próximas dos décadas, incluyendo salarios, compras y impuestos.

title: Nueva tecnología para producción de cobre metálico sin gases ni residuos
summary: Académicos de la Universidad de Concepción presentaron una nueva tecnología que permite recuperar la totalidad de los metales en concentrados de cobre sin generar gases, residuos sólidos ni líquidos. El proceso alternativo recupera además azufre, hierro y metales nobles, con una captación de CO2 de 99% y sin generar escoria.

title: Nuevo método para la obtención de cobre de forma más sostenible
summary: Investigadores de la UPM y UNIZAR han desarrollado un método hidrometalúrgico que aumenta la extracción de cobre hasta un 20% usando catalizadores de carbono derivados de biomasa. Este proceso también disminuye la movilidad del arsénico y reduce la lixiviación de azufre, contribuyendo a procesos más respetuosos con el medio ambiente.

title: Universidad de Edimburgo licencia tecnología para extraer oro y cobre de residuos electrónicos
summary: La Universidad de Edimburgo ha concedido la licencia a Lithium Universe para explotar un innovador proceso que permite una extracción más limpia de oro y cobre de residuos electrónicos. Esta tecnología representa un avance significativo en la recuperación de metales de alto valor de manera sostenible.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development in nickel markets, challenging previous pessimistic forecasts. The global nickel market is experiencing a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge, potentially ending the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025[1].

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
summary: The current price of Nickel futures is 16,796.25, with a previous close of 16,795.63. The daily trading range reflects minor volatility as the market stabilizes around the $16,800 level[2].

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
summary: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine output. This potential supply surge is influencing market sentiment and price trends[5].

title: Nickel Prices Off The Bottom
summary: Nickel prices have broken through US$10,000/t, marking a significant recovery from previous lows. A new monthly report by SNL Metals & Mining is positive on the outlook for nickel markets[9].

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026 to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision of this decision, which has already caused nickel prices to climb amid concerns over supply shortages for electric-vehicle batteries and stainless steel.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue, while reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility. Minister Bahlil Lahadalia stated the move aims to crack down on environmentally harmful operations and stabilize actual production levels that currently lag behind approved quotas.

title: Can nickel prices hit $25,000 in 2026?
summary: Nickel prices rallied to $16,500–$18,500 per ton in early 2026 as Indonesia, the world's dominant producer, signaled major supply cuts for 2026, revealing that much of the supposed surplus was a statistical illusion. The market is repricing based on the realization that only 55% of Indonesia's approved ore capacity was utilized in 2025, prompting Jakarta to shorten quota validity to one year to manage supply and pricing.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable global battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the global nickel sector.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, single-step method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon, cutting CO2 emissions by 84%. This breakthrough enables the energy-efficient processing of low-grade nickel ores, which account for 60% of total reserves, directly producing refined ferronickel alloy.

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: Nickel is identified as a critical element for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, necessitating more sustainable production methods to minimize environmental impact. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, requiring production scaling with minimal ecological footprint.

title: Indonesia's Green Nickel: From Us to the Next Generation
summary: Neo Energy shares a vision for sustainable industrialization at ICM 2026, focusing on reducing diesel dependency and lowering carbon emissions in nickel mining operations. This initiative supports the broader goal of creating a cleaner mining environment and advancing green nickel production for future generations.

title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff set-up
summary: Italy's Prysmian Group is expected to benefit from new U.S. copper tariffs that levy taxes on foreign-made cables, according to the company's CEO. The Italian cable manufacturer anticipates improved pricing and margins in the U.S. market due to these recent tariff adjustments.
